content: I'm so excited for my upcoming wedding. I've been reading a lot of books on wedding planning to get some inspiration.
That sounds like a great project. What drew you to the topic of wedding planning?

I've always been fascinated by event planning and design. I've also been interested in calligraphy and knitting for a few years now, and I see a lot of similarities between the two in terms of attention to detail.
That's really interesting. I wouldn't have thought to compare wedding planning and calligraphy. Are you planning on featuring any notable locations in your wedding?
Yeah, I'm actually planning to get married at a venue in Los Angeles, where I live. I love the scenery and the challenge of planning a wedding in a big city.
That sounds like a great idea. Los Angeles can be a really beautiful and exciting backdrop for a wedding. What made you decide to become an aerospace engineer at SpaceX?

I've always been interested in space and technology, and I found that working at SpaceX was a great way to combine those interests. I actually got my start in the field about 5 years ago, when I was working on a project with my colleague, Jack.
That's great. It's always good to have a strong foundation in the field. What do you like to do in your free time besides calligraphy and knitting?
I also enjoy cooking Thai food and working on my vintage car. I find that it helps me relax and think creatively.
That makes sense. It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that your work at SpaceX gives you a lot of opportunities to learn new things?
Yeah, I do. I've been learning a lot about new technologies and techniques, including some programming languages like Rust and Python.
That's really useful. It's always good to stay up-to-date with the latest developments in the field. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the year?

Yeah, I'm actually planning a trip to Japan in October to learn more about origami and calligraphy. I'm looking forward to spending some time with my fiancé and getting some inspiration for our wedding.
That sounds like a great idea. You deserve a break after a long year of wedding planning. Good luck with your trip and your wedding.
